chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 344 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 20.11.2019 Mr. Naveed-ul-Haq, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner Rumman Hafeez Ahmed has prayed for the grant of Succession Certificate in respect of moveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 17 & 19, which were left by his deceased father, namely, Aqil Ahmed s/o. Hafiz Hafeezullah, who allegedly died on 15.11.2018 at Karachi, leaving behind Farzana Ahmed (wife), Rumman Hafeez Ahmed (son/petitioner) and Farhan Aqil Ahmed (son) as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA and bank statements are also filed at page 13, 15 and 25 to 49. Legal heirs No. 1 & 3 have filed their Affidavits of No-Objection for the grant of Succession Certificate in favour of the petitioner, while legal heirs No. 3 has also executed Special Power of Attorney duly attested by the Notary Public at Karachi in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Sabih Saleem s/o. Saleem Anwar and Muhammad Haris Mansoob s/o. Mansoob Ahmed have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 08.09.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 26.09.2019.

Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 08.09.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 26.09.2019. 2 Today petitioner and legal heir No. 1 as well as above named witnesses are present. They affirm the contents of memo of petition as well as their affidavits and also verify their signatures made thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Succession Certificate in respect of movable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
